question: REVIEW GATE: Design 'ao review' command — a lightweight daily workflow that runs blind memos + Sigma on code changes without the full critique phase. Based on NeurIPS 2025 finding: ensemble diversity matters more than debate rounds. So for 80% of cases, blind memos + Sigma are enough. Full critique is escalation for disagreements. Concrete commands to design: (1) 'ao review' — review unstaged changes in cwd, (2) 'ao review --staged' — review staged changes, (3) 'ao review --branch main' — review all commits since diverging from main. Output should be practical: top risks, blind spots found, merge recommendation (safe/risky/needs-debate), dissent map if brains disagree. If brains agree strongly, skip critique phase (save time+tokens). If they disagree or confidence is low, auto-escalate to full debate. Design questions: How to build the diff context? Should it include file contents or just diffs? How to make output actionable vs academic? What threshold for auto-escalation to full debate? Should it save to .alpha-omega/ or be ephemeral? This is the primary daily-use command that makes AO a habit, not an event.

implementation_brief.goal: REVIEW GATE: Design 'ao review' command — a lightweight daily workflow that runs blind memos + Sigma on code changes without the full critique phase. Based on NeurIPS 2025 finding: ensemble diversity matters more than debate rounds. So for 80% of cases, blind memos + Sigma are enough. Full critique is escalation for disagreements. Concrete commands to design: (1) 'ao review' — review unstaged changes in cwd, (2) 'ao review --staged' — review staged changes, (3) 'ao review --branch main' — review all commits since diverging from main. Output should be practical: top risks, blind spots found, merge recommendation (safe/risky/needs-debate), dissent map if brains disagree. If brains agree strongly, skip critique phase (save time+tokens). If they disagree or confidence is low, auto-escalate to full debate. Design questions: How to build the diff context? Should it include file contents or just diffs? How to make output actionable vs academic? What threshold for auto-escalation to full debate? Should it save to .alpha-omega/ or be ephemeral? This is the primary daily-use command that makes AO a habit, not an event.
implementation_brief.winning_thesis: Add a separate `ao review` path with a review-specific memo schema and deterministic `review_sigma`, instead of forcing code review into the current debate/option format. Build context from `git diff --unified=3 --no-ext-diff`, `--stat`, rename metadata, and full contents only for small or newly added changed files; default to ephemeral output, and auto-escalate to full debate only when the two blind memos disagree or look weak.
constraints: Needs a new review memo schema and a dedicated resolver; the current `sigma.py` is debate-shaped and not a clean fit
Patch-only context will miss some cross-file invariants and hidden call-site breakage on large refactors
Escalation thresholds can be too noisy if they are tuned from intuition instead of repo-level evals
Branch review over large divergence may still be too big for a lightweight flow without chunking or caps
